pattern: Scanning every length-three window and keeping only the uniform-digit ones reduces the search to comparing single repeated digits, so the largest such digit names the answer.
hints: A valid answer is just one digit repeated three times, so you really only need to know the largest digit that appears three times in a row.
Slide a window of width three and check whether all three characters are identical.
Among all positions where three consecutive characters match, track the maximum repeated digit and emit it three times, or return an empty string if none exist.

edge_inputs.description: two distinct triples, pick the larger
run of four still yields a triple
later smaller run does not override an earlier larger one

stress.description: long digit strings with occasional planted runs
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(3, min(size, 1000))
    chars = [str(rng.randint(0, 9)) for _ in range(n)]
    for _ in range(rng.randint(0, 5)):
        d = str(rng.randint(0, 9))
        pos = rng.randint(0, n - 3)
        chars[pos] = chars[pos + 1] = chars[pos + 2] = d
    return [''.join(chars)]
stress.note: injects several uniform triples to stress the maximum tracking
